title: Pre-Engagement Legal Checklist
when_to_use: Before every penetration testing engagement without exception

steps.title: Written Authorisation
steps.instruction: Obtain signed written authorisation from the asset owner
steps.tip: Verbal authorisation is not enough. Get it in writing, signed by someone with authority to authorise testing

steps.title: Define Scope
steps.instruction: Define scope: exact IP ranges, domains, and URLs to be tested
steps.tip: Be specific. '192.168.1.0/24' is better than 'the internal network'. Ambiguity creates legal risk

steps.title: Document Exclusions
steps.tip: Production databases, backup systems, and third-party services are commonly excluded. Document each one

steps.title: Agree on Testing Window
steps.instruction: Agree on permitted testing hours and notification requirements
steps.tip: Some clients want testing only during business hours. Others want off-hours. Always confirm and document

steps.title: Emergency Contacts
steps.instruction: Establish emergency contacts for both teams
steps.tip: Include: client IT lead, client executive sponsor, your team lead, and an after-hours emergency number

steps.title: Data Handling Rules
steps.instruction: Confirm data handling rules: how will discovered data be stored and destroyed?
steps.tip: Encrypt all findings. Define retention period. Document destruction method. This protects both parties

steps.title: Secure Storage
steps.instruction: Store all authorisation documents in a secure, versioned location
steps.tip: Use encrypted storage. Keep copies accessible during testing. Archive after engagement completion
